title: Revamp Tornado TUI: Modal Overlays, Background Fix, Styled Menus
Create reusable Modal/Dialog component
description: Create a new internal/ui/components/modal.go with a reusable Dialog component. Reference sqlit's Dialog widget in sqlit/shared/ui/widgets_dialogs.py. Features: rounded border with BorderFocus color, optional title rendered in border, optional shortcuts subtitle (like 'Save: ^S | Cancel: <esc>'), consistent padding, background color support. Include a ModalOverlay helper that centers content and overlays on top of a base view. Add styles to styles.go for Modal, ModalTitle, ModalSubtitle.

title: Refactor connection screen to use modal overlay
description: Modify internal/ui/screens/connect.go to show connection form as a modal overlay on StateWelcome instead of replacing the entire screen. When space is pressed, show the modal on top of the logo/animation. The modal should use the new Dialog component with title 'Connect to Database' and shortcuts like 'Connect: <enter> | Cancel: <esc>'. Remove StateForm as a separate full-screen state - instead render the modal on top of viewWelcome(). Keep StateConnecting as is (full screen spinner). Clean up viewForm() to render within modal dimensions (smaller, centered).

title: Fix browser screen full background rendering
description: Fix internal/ui/screens/browser.go background issue where it only covers the explore section. The background should cover the entire terminal. In View(), ensure the final output has a background style applied to fill the whole screen. Currently line 293 has 'lipgloss.NewStyle().Background(styles.BgDefault).Render(framed)' but this may not cover all empty space. Also ensure individual panes have proper background colors that respect the current theme. Check renderPane() method - the custom border drawing may not fill background properly. Consider using lipgloss.NewStyle().Background() for each pane's content area.

title: Create styled space/leader menu overlay
description: Replace the current leaderActive footer text approach in internal/ui/screens/browser.go with a proper modal menu overlay. When space is pressed, show a Dialog modal in the bottom-right corner (like sqlit's LeaderMenuScreen which uses 'align: right bottom'). The menu should show categorized commands: 'Explorer: e', 'Maximize: f', 'Connect: c', etc. Use the new Modal component with title 'Commands' and subtitle 'Close: <esc>'. Remove the leaderActive state and statusMsg approach. The menu should dismiss on any key press that selects a command or on esc.

title: Update theme selector to match modal style
description: Refactor the themeMenu in internal/ui/screens/browser.go to use the new Modal/Dialog component. Currently it uses a simple overlayBox approach. Update overlayThemeMenu() to use the Dialog component for consistent styling with the connection modal and leader menu. The theme list should appear inside a styled dialog with title 'Themes' and subtitle 'Select: <enter> | Cancel: <esc>'.

title: Add tests and verify theme integration
description: Add tests for the new modal component in internal/ui/components/modal_test.go. Update connect_test.go to verify modal overlay behavior. Test that all three modals (connection, leader menu, theme selector) properly apply theme colors when the theme changes. Ensure background colors update across all components. Run the application and verify: 1) Connection modal appears over logo, 2) Background fills entire terminal in browser, 3) Space menu is a styled box, 4) All modals share consistent styling.
